subject: These OOM changes are related AAF Integration
commit_message:
These OOM changes are related AAF Integration

Here we have the ability to optionally disable AAF integration.
A global variable global.security.aaf.enabled=true
will turn on AAF security. with global.security.aaf.enabled=false
it will use spring.security to ensure backward compatibilty. updated
based on review comments

Issue-ID: SO-2452
Signed-off-by: Ramesh Parthasarathy(rp6768)<ramesh.parthasarathy@att.com>
Change-Id: Ia83622ad681cfd122ee906ccd1654b10b5e31fe4

Patch Set 10: Code-Review-2

Hey Ramesh,

I do not see the ability to optionally disable AAF integration. This is expected as part of the M# security checklist. Other applications such as AAI, CLAMP already have a configuration value to do this. Since this does not yet exist in this helm chart please refer to  https://jira.onap.org/browse/OOM-1986 for the general global configuration value we would like to align all projects to.

Thanks,
Mike.

Hi Mike,
This requirement is never communicated before. This means I will have to reconsider all the changes made so far. We use different of credentials for current basic authentication and AAF. This would mean, I think, two separate set of servlet filters will be in place and the specific filter would be activated based on the profile used. The value file will need to provide either basic authentication credentials or AAF credentials based on the profile. I am not sure, at this time, how one single global.security.aaf.enabled = false would fit into the above layout. Let me see what I can do about it.
